bienvenida.lead: Thirty years painting Córdoba from the inside. His pictures are not postcards: they are neighbourhood memory, courtyard light and the exact shadow a cape leaves when it falls.
bienvenida.meta: 13 stops · Old Quarter · ~2 hours on foot
bienvenida.premio: Complete the route and get a 30 % discount on selected artworks.

salas.viana_gancho: Landscape and local colour. A palace with twelve courtyards and five hundred years of secrets.
salas.casa12pb_gancho: The styles of Pineda. This is the second hall: the one that changes everything you thought you knew.
salas.inaudita_gancho: The dangers of bullfighting. The last hall. Where it all began.

escaner.instruccion: Point the camera at the QR code at the entrance
escaner.error_token: QR not recognised. Make sure you scan the official route sign.
escaner.error_ya_sellada: This stop is already stamped in your passport.

intermedia.instruccion:
When you arrive
open your phone camera
and point it at the Pineda sign
that you'll find at the entrance
intermedia.anzuelo_0_1: A palace with twelve courtyards and five hundred years of secrets. Pineda came here and saw all of Córdoba in a single whitewashed wall.
intermedia.anzuelo_1_2: The Santa Marina neighbourhood smells of bullfighting. Manolete was born two streets away. Pineda painted him without painting him: the shadow of a cape is enough.
intermedia.anzuelo_2_3: Since 1850, guitarists and poets have been drinking at La Fuenseca without anyone having invited them. The shows here are not scheduled: they improvise.
intermedia.anzuelo_3_4: Here is the second hall. The one that changes everything you thought you knew about how Pineda paints.
intermedia.anzuelo_4_5: Since 1880, El Pisto has seen Manolete, Julio Romero de Torres and the Córdoba rogue pass through. They all drank at the same bar.
intermedia.anzuelo_5_6: Some taverns serve wine. Others keep paintings the owner won't sell at any price.
intermedia.anzuelo_6_7: The Jewish Quarter cannot be explained: it must be walked. Every cobblestone has three civilisations beneath it. Pineda painted them all at once.
intermedia.anzuelo_7_8: A 15th-century house next to the Royal Stables. It smells of Córdoba courtyards and home cooking.
intermedia.anzuelo_8_9: San Basilio is the neighbourhood of courtyards. The gate that closes it to the south has been letting in those who know how to look for centuries.
intermedia.anzuelo_9_10: They say La Viuda has the coldest salmorejo in Córdoba and the hardest painting on the route to find. See for yourself.
intermedia.anzuelo_10_11: Rodríguez Marín. The street where the old quarter ends and something quieter, more neighbourhood-like begins. Pineda painted it exactly like that.
intermedia.anzuelo_11_12: Almost there. Two doors from La Inaudita there is a cazuela that carries the name of the trade that filled this street for centuries.
intermedia.anzuelo_12_13: The last hall. Where it all began. Here is the most important work, and here is where you redeem the coupon.

cupon.instruccion: Show this code at the till at La Inaudita
cupon.aviso_domingo: ⚠️ La Inaudita is closed on Sundays. You can redeem it Monday–Saturday, 10:00–14:00 and 18:00–20:30.
cupon.fine_print: Valid until 30 May 2025 · Single use · Not combinable
cupon.rgpd_texto_1: I agree that La Inaudita may use my email to send me the coupon and cultural communications. I can exercise my rights at
